# XCON Viewer LLM Guide

XCON Viewer is a public viewer for XCON UI documents. XCON describes screens as structured objects built from names, values, arrays, and dictionaries. The same screen can be represented as XCON/SKETCH, XCON/JSON, XCON/XML, or XCON/TAGLESS.

## Core Guidance

- XCON Viewer is viewer-only.
- XCON renders declarative UI documents without executing application behavior.
- Prefer XCON/SKETCH when generating Markdown-facing examples and LLM drafts.
- Use XCON/JSON for canonical structure, APIs, schema validation, and interchange.
- Use XCON/XML only when compatibility with XML tools, comments, metadata, or round-trip editing is the topic.
- Use XCON/TAGLESS only for transport-friendly symbolic serialization and marker compatibility examples.
- Do not invent event handlers, backend calls, database sections, or behavior logic in public XCON examples.

## Public URLs

- Home: https://xconviewer.dev/
- Docs: https://xconviewer.dev/docs
- Spec: https://xconviewer.dev/spec
- Examples: https://xconviewer.dev/examples
- Live Editor: https://xconviewer.dev/play
- SKETCH Editor: https://xconviewer.dev/play/sketch
- Markdown Playground: https://xconviewer.dev/play/markdown
- XCON Chain: https://xconviewer.dev/xcon-chain/
- XCON Workflow: https://xconviewer.dev/xcon-workflow/
- Template Studio: https://xconviewer.dev/template-lab
- Meta Lab: https://xconviewer.dev/meta-lab
- API: https://xconviewer.dev/api
- Security: https://xconviewer.dev/security
- History: https://xconviewer.dev/history
- FAQ: https://xconviewer.dev/faq
- JSON Schema: https://xconviewer.dev/xcon.schema.json
- Sitemap: https://xconviewer.dev/sitemap.xml

## Syntax Forms

XCON/SKETCH is the compact authoring syntax for Markdown examples and LLM-generated drafts. It parses into the same Object Model and can be converted to JSON first, then XML or TAGLESS when compatibility output is needed.

XCON/JSON is the canonical structure for APIs, schema validation, and interchange.

XCON/XML is a compatibility syntax for UI tools, comments, metadata, and designer integration. Semantic XML components should use child component name attributes when a round trip through SKETCH, JSON, XML, and TAGLESS needs stable keys.

XCON/TAGLESS is a symbolic compatibility syntax for transport-friendly serialization. Marker sets can be selected at the document level to reduce delimiter conflicts. If marker characters appear in data, escape them or choose another marker set.

## Template Studio

Template Studio combines Markdown document shells, JSON fixture data, XCON Chain SUGAR expressions, XCON/SKETCH visual blocks, and XCON Workflow sketch blocks. Use it for data-bound Markdown UI documents such as invoices, bulletins, reports, campaign briefs, dashboards, and operating plans. Keep visible authoring examples SKETCH + SUGAR first; JSON should appear as fixture data, not as the primary authoring language.

## Meta Lab

Meta Lab experiments with path-addressed metadata contracts that can drive fixture selection, SUGAR data binding, XCON/SKETCH visual blocks, and XCON Workflow blocks from the same catalog. Use it to model reusable document contracts where a metadata path selects both the business data shape and the rendered workflow/dashboard pattern.

## Minimal XCON/SKETCH Example

```xcon-sketch
screen 402x300 bg #F8FAFC
  title: label "Hello XCON" at 24 32 354 34
    font 24 800
    color #111827
```

## Minimal XCON/JSON Example

```json
{
  "type": "form",
  "pos": [0, 0, 402, 300],
  "backgroundColor": "#F8FAFC",
  "components": {
    "title": {
      "type": "label",
      "pos": [24, 32, 354, 34],
      "text": "Hello XCON",
      "font": { "size": 24, "weight": 800 },
      "color": "#111827"
    }
  }
}
```

## Packages

- @xcon-viewer/core: Object Model, parser, serializer, converter, and validator.
- @xcon-viewer/viewer: Secure renderer and Web Component.
- @xcon-viewer/cli: validate, convert, format, and render commands.
- @xcon-viewer/react: React wrapper for the Web Component.
- @xcon-viewer/vue: Vue wrapper for the Web Component.
- @xcon-viewer/markdown-it: markdown-it plugin for XCON fences.
- @xcon-viewer/remark: remark plugin for XCON code nodes.
- @xcon-viewer/vite-plugin: imports .xcon, .xcon.xml, .xcon.json, .xcon.sketch, and .xcons as modules.
- @xcon-viewer/github-action: validates and renders XCON code blocks in CI.

## Security Boundary

Allowed: structured screen data, public component properties, safe inline styles, local resource URLs, and design metadata needed for rendering.

Blocked: JavaScript execution, event handlers, backend/database sections, unsafe URLs, raw HTML injection by default, and behavior execution.

## Example Sources

Use https://xconviewer.dev/examples as the public entry point. The repository also contains generated examples under examples/*. Each example normally includes:

- *.xcon.sketch
- *.xcon.json
- *.xcon.xml
- *.xcon
- README.md

## Component Guidance

Use the component reference at https://xconviewer.dev/docs for the detailed public property list. Keep property names LLM-friendly and CSS-adjacent where appropriate, such as backgroundColor, color, font, border, shadow, padding, margin, direction, alignment, items, components, and content.

Unknown properties should be treated as strings unless the public property spec defines a clearer type.
